expectations.text: The OWASP-style jti denylist revocation model is not reported as a failure
Report acknowledges rotation and reuse detection with family revocation are present and correct
expectations.evidence: Credited jti denylist, family-wide revocation on reuse, fail-closed Redis checks, single-flight client

expectations.text: Report notes at least one real Tier 3 gap
expectations.evidence: Found missing rate limiting on login and refresh; also noted cookie hardening options

Also found the genuine password-change revocation bug (only the presenting family is revoked). Additional findings (missing package.json deps, redis connect() never called) are true observations about the fixture snapshot

notes: Strong baseline. Graded B+ despite finding the same password-change bug the skill graded C for; the skill's rubric is more conservative and more reproducible (confirmed Tier 2 failure caps at C by rule rather than reviewer mood). Baseline mixed non-auth operational issues (unhandled promise rejections) into the security list.
